title: Why US struck Iran amid shaky ceasefire: 10 things to know about the 'self-defence' strikes
content: US forces struck Iranian missile sites and boats near the Strait of Hormuz. This happened during peace talks aimed at ending a three-month conflict. Iran accused the US of a war crime. Reports mentioned explosions and fatalities on Larak Island. Discussions continue on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's uranium stockpile.

title: What can happen to Iran’s uranium? Trump lays out options for turning it into 'nuclear dust'
content: US forces conducted 'self-defense' strikes on Iranian missile sites and boats near the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions despite ongoing diplomatic talks. President Trump warned Iran on enriched uranium, demanding its surrender or destruction. Iran condemned the strikes as a 'war crime,' accusing the US of targeting civilians. Peace negotiations continue, reportedly discussing the Strait's reopening and a ceasefire extension.

title: From Z-plus security to stripped surveillance: Why cops landed at Abhishek Banerjee’s residence
content: Kolkata police visited Abhishek Banerjee's Shantiniketan residence. Plainclothes officers removed a monitor and hard disks. This follows a reduction in Banerjee's security cover. Trinamool claims it was a routine removal of security equipment. Police stated it was a standard official exercise. The incident has sparked speculation.

title: IPL Qualifier 1: Away from Kohli vs Gill spotlight, Bhuvi vs Siraj may decide the contest
content: Amid the Virat Kohli–Shubman Gill spotlight and Dharamsala’s batting-friendly conditions, IPL Qualifier 1 could be decided not by the big hitters but by the bowlers. With both Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bengaluru boasting disciplined and in-form attacks featuring Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Mohammed Siraj, Kagiso Rabada and Rashid Khan, the battle for early breakthroughs and composure in key overs may determine who books a direct ticket to the final.

title: Fuel shock: How price hike changes what you eat, what you buy and how you travel
content: Fuel prices have risen sharply, adding to household expenses and transportation costs. This surge, driven by global events, is straining supply chains and impacting everyday goods. Oil marketing companies are seeing stock gains. The government faces a balancing act between consumer relief and revenue. The economic outlook remains a focus amidst these challenges.

title: How Toyota along with Alabama high school is solving one of US biggest problems
content: Huntsville's Center for Technology is equipping students with vital skills for industrial maintenance and engineering roles, jobs industry leaders deem AI-resistant.  Toyota Alabama's $1 million investment fuels this program, offering lucrative careers with minimal debt.  This initiative addresses a critical US shortage of skilled trades, a concern echoed by CEOs from Ford, Lowe's, and BlackRock.

title: Rajat Patidar: RCB's captain cool, whose strength lies in his calmness
content: From being released by Royal Challengers Bengaluru to returning as a mid-season replacement and eventually leading the franchise to its maiden IPL title, Rajat Patidar's journey has been one of persistence and patience. Built on calmness rather than flamboyance, the 32-year-old has grown into a composed leader who now stands two wins away from joining an elite list of IPL captains with back-to-back titles.

title: Why Delhi Gymkhana Club’s crisis feels personal for generations of members
content: Long-time members of Delhi Gymkhana Club describe it as a second home, deeply integrated into family life, friendships, and daily routines for decades. The institution fostered a sense of community and belonging, offering a cherished space for recreation, personal refuge, and cultural expression.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system'
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: After 5 deaths at Kota, Rajasthan bans childbirth drug TOCIN ,sample fails potency test
content: Rajasthan's Drug Control Department has banned TOCIN (Oxytocin Injection 5 ml) manufactured by Jackson Laboratories Pvt Ltd after a sample failed quality tests due to insufficient oxytocin. Approximately 3,500 vials have been seized. The department clarified that this banned injection is not directly linked to recent maternal deaths in Kota, as those cases involved caesarean deliveries.

title: No place for Kohli as Sooryavanshi gets the nod in ex-CSK star's best XII
content: Former India batter Subramaniam Badrinath picked his IPL 2026 league-stage best XII, leaving out Virat Kohli and KL Rahul. He chose Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Shubman Gill as openers, with Sai Sudharsan, Heinrich Klaasen, and Rajat Patidar in the batting lineup. His bowling-heavy squad included Krunal Pandya, Sunil Narine, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Jofra Archer, and Kagiso Rabada.

title: Dera chief Ram Rahim gets 16th parole, walks out of Rohtak jail on 30-day furlough
content: Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh has received his 16th parole, a 30-day release from Sunaria Jail. This latest furlough follows his acquittal in the journalist murder case earlier this year.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Rabindra Dhant chases historic UFC deal; two wins from contract
content: Nepal has never had a UFC fighter. Rabindra Dhant is now closer than anyone before him. Ahead of his Road to UFC quarterfinal in Galaxy Arena of Macau, the 27-year-old reflects on hardship, missed opportunities, and the mindset that has kept him grounded through the biggest moment of his career.

title: 'Maybe in another life I won’t fight cancer in my 20s': 27-yr-old’s hard lesson
content: Radhika Sapra, 27, battles stage 4 ovarian cancer alone after losing her father and facing a childhood of abuse. Despite immense financial struggles and the cancer's recurrence, she finds strength in her partner, aunt, and a loyal friend. Her indomitable spirit shines through, a testament to hope amidst adversity.

title: Minnesota Vikings, Dallas Cowboys linked in surprise Linebacker trade rumors
content: Blake Cashman has emerged as a potential trade candidate as the Minnesota Vikings enter the final year of his contract. The Dallas Cowboys have been suggested as a possible landing spot, with analysts believing a Day 3 draft pick could get a deal done. Despite the speculation, Cashman remains one of Minnesota's most productive defenders after recording 144 tackles in 13 games last season, making a trade far from certain.

title: Blake Lively ‘convinced’ Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce won’t snub her wedding invite
content: Blake Lively is reportedly optimistic that her friendship with Taylor Swift can recover after the Justin Baldoni controversy and settlement. Insiders claim Lively already has a dress prepared for Swift’s future wedding because she believes she will still be invited. The rumors reignited debate online about tension inside Swift’s celebrity circle. While some sources say Taylor remains cautious about her guest list, others believe removing the legal drama could create space for reconciliation between the longtime friends moving forward.

title: Tyreek Hill shocks fans with new career move amid NFL free agency silence
content: Free agent wide receiver Tyreek Hill has embraced DJing in Miami while recovering from knee surgery. He finds enjoyment in EDM music. Despite being linked to several teams for several months since his release from the Miami Dolphins, no NFL franchise has signed him as of yet. Hill’s new hobby could potentially enhance his football skills. The incident mirrors the positive impact seen with Bijan Robinson’s DJing experience in 2024.

title: Fourth CNG price rise: IGL hikes rates by Rs 2 across NCR region
content: Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL) has announced another Re 1 per kg hike in CNG prices, marking the fourth increase in under two weeks. This brings Delhi's CNG rate to Rs 80.09 per kg.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock market recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research at Mirae Asset ShareKhan recommends Larsen & Toubro (L&T), Bajaj Finance, and Bank of India as the top stocks to consider buying on May 26, 2026. He shares the range for buy calls, stop loss and target levels:

title: Norway Chess: Divya stuns world champion; Carlsen handed shock defeat
content: India enjoyed a decent opening day at Norway Chess as 20-year-old debutante Divya Deshmukh held World Champion Ju Wenjun to a classical draw before defeating her in the Armageddon tiebreak. Joining her in the winner's circle were D Gukesh and R Praggnanandhaa, who also won their respective Armageddon matches, while Magnus Carlsen suffered a shock defeat.

title: 'Drishyam 3' box office collection day 5: Mohanlal starrer mints over Rs 156 crore worldwide
content: Mohanlal and Jeethu Joseph's 'Drishyam 3' has achieved a remarkable worldwide box office collection of over Rs 156 crore within its first five days. Despite a notable drop on its first Monday, the suspense thriller has demonstrated strong performance, with India's net collection reaching Rs 61.80 crore.

title: Punjab civic polls today across 103 urban bodies; over 7,500 candidates in fray
content: Punjab gears up for polling in 103 urban local bodies today, featuring 7,555 candidates vying for 1,896 wards.  Eight municipal corporations, 75 municipal councils, and 20 nagar panchayats are part of this extensive electoral exercise.  Over 35 lakh voters will cast their ballots, with counting scheduled for May 29.

title: 'I pity you guys': Shreyas Iyer's sister blasts online abuse
content: Shresta Iyer, sister of cricketer Shreyas Iyer, responded to online trolling after Punjab Kings missed the IPL 2026 playoffs. An old celebratory “banter” reel resurfaced, leading to abuse targeting her, her workplace, colleagues, and family. Shresta said the video was harmless fun, not hate, and urged trolls to stop harassing people connected to her personally and professionally.

title: Kerala HSE 12th result 2026 today: Check list of official websites to download plus two result
content: The Directorate of Higher Secondary Education (DHSE), Kerala, will release the Plus Two result for 2026 today, May 26, with scores accessible from 3 pm. Approximately 4,25,437 students appeared for the Class 12 examinations, which concluded on March 28. Students can download their provisional scorecards online via official websites and other platforms.

title: Oil prices remain mixed as fresh US strikes on Iran dim peace hopes
content: Oil prices fluctuated as US-Iran talks showed signs of progress, clashing with US military strikes on Iranian missile sites and vessels. Hopes for the Strait of Hormuz reopening were dampened by fresh clashes, adding to market uncertainty after a major energy shock.

title: Kohli's IPL playoff record becomes major talking point before Qualifier 1
content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru have secured a playoff spot in IPL 2026, driven by Virat Kohli's stellar performance. Despite his overall brilliance, Kohli's knockout stage record presents a potential concern for RCB as they aim for back-to-back titles, facing Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1.

title: Mass resignations rock Bengal civic bodies, deepen strain within Trinamool
content: Bengal's civic bodies are facing collapse due to widespread resignations, even within Trinamool strongholds, signaling internal party strain.  Recent mass departures in North Barrackpore, Diamond Harbour, and Contai have destabilized numerous municipalities.  Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has appealed for unity amidst this growing unrest.

title: Qualifier 1, IPL: From Virat Kohli to Shubman Gill, players to watch out for
content: The IPL playoffs commence with a high-octane Qualifier 1 between defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans. Key players like Virat Kohli and Shubman Gill are set to dazzle. All-rounders Krunal Pandya and Jason Holder, alongside potent bowlers Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Rashid Khan, will be crucial for their teams' success in this crucial encounter.

title: Watch: Ranveer Singh makes 1st public appearance amid FWICE boycott
content: Bollywood star Ranveer Singh made his first public appearance after FWICE issued a Non-Cooperation Directive against him over his reported exit from 'Don 3'. The filmmakers allege Rs 45 crore loss due to his last-minute departure. Singh, maintaining silence, stated the issues are contractual and require legal adjudication, while FWICE remains open to an amicable resolution.

title: Quad Foreign Ministers meet LIVE: Indo-Pacific region must remain a driver of global growth, says Jaishankar
content: The New Delhi meeting will be attended by U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong and Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi, with External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar presiding over it

title: Arrests, mass resignations, ‘bulldozer action’ deepen Trinamool’s crisis across West Bengal
content: Drives against illegal construction, notices to properties linked to senior Trinamool leaders, and mass resignations in local bodies pile pressure on the TMC three weeks after the BJP assumed power in West Bengal

title: CNG prices hiked fourth time in eleven days in Delhi and North India; latest by ₹2/kg
content: CNG in the National Capital territory of Delhi now costs ₹83.09 per kg; prices have effectively risen ₹6 per kilogram spanning across four tranches

title: Heavy rain in Bagalkot, Vijayapura, Belagavi in north Karnataka, crops damaged
content: Fire fighters sought the assistance of local residents in pumping out water from Vasavi Hospital in Muddebihal of Vijayapura district

title: Thoothukudi schoolgirl sexual assault and murder case: Man gets death penalty on two counts
content: The girl had gone missing in a scrub forest area around 250 metres from her residence on the evening of March 10; she was found murdered the next day

title: ₹10 overcharge: At TASMAC outlets, low-paid workers bear cost of administrative failure
content: Before the elections, TVK chief C. Joseph Vijay, during a rally, took a jibe at the extra ₹10 charged at TASMAC outlets. Workers at the shops do not dispute the practice, but say it merely offsets irregularities in the system

title: Glitches in CBSE portal leave Class 12 students seeking re-evaluation hassled
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has sought a detailed report from CBSE, deputed technical team from IIT Madras and IIT Kanpur to assist the board in solving tech issues

title: Canada PM Carney’s visit to India reset ties after 2023 killing of Sikh activist, Piyush Goyal says
content: The ties were strained under Mark Carney's predecessor in the wake of the 2023 killing of Sikh activist Hareep Singh Nijjar in Canada

title: President Droupadi Murmu confers 66 Padma awards
content: President Droupadi Murmu confers the Padma Vibhushan on legendary Bollywood actor Dharmendra posthumously and violin virtuoso N. Rajam for exceptional contribution to their respective fields

title: T.N. government announces cooperative crop loan waiver up to ₹50,000
content: As the loan amount increases, the quantum of the amount waived will decrease; small and marginal farmers who borrowed more than ₹1 lakh will be provided a flat waiver of ₹5,000

title: Neelam Meena appointed Chief Electoral Officer of West Bengal
content: Senior IAS officer to take charge immediately; outgoing CEO Manoj Kumar Agarwal was appointed Chief Secretary of the State

title: M.P. CM announces financial aid to people died in Bhojshala movement; govt to build Saraswati corridor, research centre
content: Mr. Yadav also announced construction of a ‘Saraswati Lok’ corridor and establishment of a research centre in the name of King Bhoja while terming the Bhojshala verdict the “result of a 750-year-long struggle”

title: CBI takes over probe in Twisha Sharma death case
content: After the meeting, the CBI re-registered the state police FIR as its own case, according to the procedure

title: Astronomers spot blue straggler star hosting brown dwarf companion
content: This breakthrough by the team from the Indian Institute of Astrophysics, Aryabhatta Research Institute of Observational Sciences, Gauhati University, and INAF-Catania Astrophysical Observatory, Italy, could reshape astronomers’ understanding of how stars evolve

title: Government polytechnics in Tamil Nadu record near total placement this year
content: According to officials, a reason for this could be the very nature of the diploma courses, which equip students with more hands-on, vocational, and trade-specific skills

title: P&T apartment residents to lay indefinite siege to GCDA office
content: Residents have been protesting the persistent leakage since 78 families moved into the apartments built by GCDA in January 2024

title: Horse trading at horse speed: DMK chief Stalin on AIADMK MLAs joining TVK
content: Three MLAs who were part of the S.P. Velumani faction of the AIADMK met Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar and submitted their resignation letters, before joining the TVK

title: DSEL issues order to increase egg distribution coverage in Karnataka schools to 90%
content: The DSEL has instructed officials to create awareness among students about the benefits of egg consumption, and has ordered that the target of 80% egg distribution should be achieved by September 2026.

title: Intense speculation as Siddaramaiah and Shivakumar head to Delhi for meeting on May 26
content: Though Congress General Secretary in-charge of Karnataka Randeep Singh Surjewala said it would be a consultation meeting ahead of the elections to Rajya Sabha and Legislative Council, speculation on the leadership issue has refused to die down

title: Major fire at oil godown in Manali
content: Officials said the fire intensified as oil cans stored inside the warehouse exploded, making it difficult for personnel to approach the site initially

title: Madhu lynching case: Kerala HC enhances sentence of 12 accused to life imprisonment, acquits one
content: Madhu’s mother and the Kerala government had filed appeals seeking enhanced punishment for the accused. The mentally challenged tribal youth was killed following the allegation that he had stolen rice from a grocery shop in February 2018

title: Oil PSUs report sharp rise in demand for diesel, petrol in Telangana
content: Diesel sales up by 12.6%, petrol by 9% in first three weeks of May amid intensified agricultural, harvesting activities, retail consumers of other suppliers increasingly shifting towards PSUs outlet due to competitive pricing

title: Twisha Sharma death: SC pained by handling of case, asks media to exercise restraint; CBI team dispatched to Bhopal
content: Better to have a divorced daughter than face such an unfortunate incident, says Solicitor General during SC hearing

title: Farmers are an integral part of alco-bev industry, do not leave them behind: Timmapur urges industry
content: Most raw materials required for breweries — including barley, grains, fruits, and other agricultural produce — are cultivated by farmers. The government is committed to grow the industry beyond Bengaluru

title: Over 300 tourists stranded in Gulmarg’s high-altitude cable cars rescued
content: Panic gripped the tourists stranded in 65 gondolas after a technical slag, even as the weather turned inclement, with strong winds and rains lashing Gulmarg; the rescue operation lasted over four hours; 15 SDRF teams were deployed

title: ‘Rape, murder’ of minor girl sparks outrage in Kashmir, one arrested
content: The victim, who is believed to be around 12, went missing on Saturday and her body was recovered on Sunday

title: NIA conducts searches of banned Jamaat-e-Islami’s trusts, associations in Srinagar, Shopian
content: During its investigations, the NIA said, it had emerged that the JeI had been actively involved in collecting terror funds, and fuelling terrorism in the Kashmir Valley and other parts of India

title: Inclusion of Maithili in CBSE curriculum will connect future generation to their roots: Bihar CM
content: BJP leader and MP from Darbhanga Parliamentary seat, Gopal Ji Thakur, met Union Education Minister and Minister of State for Education, and demanded the inclusion of Maithili language as a subject in the CBSE curriculum.

title: Naidu calls for blue economy vision plan for Andhra Pradesh coastline
content: Chief Minister directs officials to prepare a comprehensive strategy focusing on sustainable coastal development, marine resource utilisation, aquaculture value addition and ecological protection

title: Three AIADMK MLAs resign from posts in Tamil Nadu, join TVK
content: Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar accepts their resignations. AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami and AMMK founder T.T.V. Dhinakaran accuse TVK of horse trading.

title: "I'm Jealous Of Vijay": Pawan Kalyan Quips He Roamed Streets For 15 Years
content: Vijay, who launched his party TVK in 2024, secured 108 seats and became the largest party in 2026 Tamil Nadu elections.

title: Students Protest At Gauhati University Over Fee Hike, Admission Charges
content: Students protest fee hike and demand free admission at Gauhati University, blocking national highway.

title: Live Updates | "We Have To Address Connectivity Choke Points Issues": S Jaishankar To Quad Leaders
content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

title: Canada "Working Fast" For "Game Changer" Trade Deal With India
content: Canada and India have been in trade talks since 2010. Talks were shut down by Ottawa in 2023 after Canadian authorities alleged that India was involved in the killing of Canadian Sikh activist Hardeep Singh Nijjar near Vancouver in June that year.

title: 'I Lost My Daughter, Mamata Banerjee  Lost Her Chair': RG Kar Victim's Mother
content: BJP MLA Ratna Debnath met Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari seeking justice for her daughter's RG Kar rape-murder case.

title: Madhya Pradesh's Dhar To Get Maa Saraswati Lok, Raja Bhoj Research Institute
content: This comes days after the Madhya Pradesh High Court declared the disputed Bhojshala complex a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati.

title: "Open Sale Of Drugs In Punjab Has Stopped": Bhagwant Mann To NDTV
content: Bhagwant Mann said the open sale of drugs in Punjab has completely stopped following the launch of the 'War on Drugs' campaign, and only cross-border covert operations exist now.

title: US Attacks Iran's Bandar Abbas, Then A 'Uranium' Warning From Trump
content: Tehran is yet to respond to the latest US strikes, and it remains unclear if the new American agression will have any impact on a potential peace agreement between the two warring nations.

title: US-Iran War LIVE: US Says Hormuz Strait Has To Open 'One Way Or Other' After Bandar Abbas Strikes
content: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

title: "Turn Over Nuclear Dust": Trump Warns Iran As US Conducts Fresh Strikes
content: Donald Trump's warning came as the US forces conducted "self-defence" strikes on Iranian boats and missile launch facilities.

title: US Conducts 'Self-Defence' Strikes On Iranian Boats, Missile Launch Sites
content: US Central Command said in a statement the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces."

title: 2nd Group Of Australian Women Linked To ISIS To Return Home
content: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said the government was not assisting their travel and that any who have committed crimes "can expect to face the full force of the law".

title: Speeding SUV Rams 60-Year-Old Watchman In Delhi After Verbal Spat: Cops
content: A 60-year-old watchman in Outer Delhi's Peeragarhi village was killed after an SUV rammed him multiple times. Police called it a "well-planned" murder carried out over a verbal spat.

title: UP Drunk Father Kills Son, Hides Body Inside House; Arrested: Cops
content: A 30-year-old man was allegedly killed by his father after a drunken argument in Uttar Pradesh's Lakhimpur Kheri, police said on Monday.

title: 6.9 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Chile, No Injuries Reported
content: A magnitude 6.9 earthquake struck northern Chile on Monday, the US Geological Survey (USGS) reported.

title: Trump Says Iran's Enriched Uranium Will Be "Turned Over" To US Or Destroyed
content: Trump said this decision will be taken in "conjunction and collaboration" with Iran, signalling Tehran's relaxing its stand as the two countries attempt to reach a peace agreement.

title: Indians prefer cereals, neglect other food items: ICMR study
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: The Abraham Accord plus: Trump aims for grand bargain tying Islamic world with Israel
content: the sudden and sweeping proposal, unveiled in a lengthy Truth Social post, stunned diplomats across the Middle East and South Asia . the proposal triggered resistance from Pakistan and unease in Riyadh and doha . "it should be mandatory that all these countries simultaneously, at a minimum, simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords," Trump said .

title: Tribal woman walks 4km with 90-yr-old mom-in-law on back, for KYC in C'garh district
content: Rashmi is a Special Correspondent with The Times of India in Chhattisgarh. She covers Politics, Left Wing Extremism, Crime and Human Rights among other areas of news value.

title: Blow to AIADMK: 3 legislators quit, join Tamil Nadu CM Vijay’s outfit within hours
content: three MLAs from C Ve Shanmugam-S P Velumani-led rebel camp submitted their resignations to assembly speaker J C D Prabhakar on Monday . they direct headed to meet PWD minister Aadhav Arjuna at his chamber on the second floor of the secretariat to join Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) the trio were among the 25 who had voted in favour of chief minister C Joseph Vijay-led TVK during the trust vote on may 13 .

title: Assam seeks to become 4th state with UCC as bill tabled in assembly
content: Kangkan Kalita is a reporter with The Times of India and covers issues on health, education, stories of human interest while keeping a close watch on political developments and student movements. Reporting on environment and forest related issues and concerns of the northeast interest him equally. Read More

title: Govt wrote thrice to Delhi Gymkhana since Sept last year to clear Rs 48-crore rent
content: the land and development office had written three letters to the club management since sept last year to clear around Rs 48 crore in rent that DGC owed to it . some club members said they were not intimated about the pending due earlier . there was response to queries sent to two govt-appointed GC members on why the due was not paid .

title: Eye on China, Quad to ramp up critical minerals cooperation
content: the foreign ministers' meeting will take place at a time prospects for a summit in india of the diplomatic partnership . the key takeaway from the meeting will be an important step towards countering china's dominance of critical minerals and thwarting any possible weaponisation of its resources.

title: With 4th hike in 11 days, petrol crosses Rs 100 a litre in capital
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. Read More

title: CBI registers case in Twisha Sharma death, takes over probe from Bhopal police
content: An investigative journalist covering internal security, terrorism, military and strategic affairs, intelligence and investigative agencies, anti-corruption institutions, geopolitics, wildlife, environment, and governance. He is known for exclusive, deeply reported stories that explore the intersection of security, conservation, conflict, policy, and power informed by field investigations, ground reportage, and cross-border perspectives. Read More

title: Parents' fear of divorce stigma may be pushing many women into death traps: SC
content: apex court awarded life imprisonment to a man for murdering his wife for dowry . he said parents' anxiety to save the marriage of their daughters may push many women into a death trap by sending them back to matrimonial homes . the court noted that soma had repeatedly informed her parents about the harassment she faced .

title: 'Don't be sentimental': CJI Surya Kant brushes aside plea over ‘Cockroach Janta Party’
content: a bench led by CJI Surya Kant brushed aside the issue by advising him, "Don't take it so sentimentally" he had clarified that he respected youth of india a day after his 'cockroach' remark went viral and led to setting up a satirical Cockroach Janta Party . a lawyer's arguments accused Dipke of attempting to denigrate and defame the judiciary .

title: ‘No lesson learnt from past NEET mess’: SC slams NTA, to monitor process
content: the apex court pulled up the national testing agency over the paper leak issue . it said no lesson had been learned and mistakes were being repeated . a bench of justices decided to examine the process followed by NTA . in 2024, the court had raised serious questions on NTA's functioning .

title: India nears 114 Rafales deal: ‘LoR for mega contract ready, to be sent to France soon’
content: the 4.5-generation multirole jets are expected to be shipped to the u.s. in a few weeks . it is a formal govt-to-govt document used to initiate major defence acquisitions under international frameworks like foreign military sales .

title: 'Wouldn't be here if Trump didn't want me to': Rubio on US prez's 'hellhole' remark
content: "he cares - he wouldn't have sent someone like Sergio (Gor) to be our ambassador," says Rubio . he said there are stupid people everywhere who say dumb things . the travelling US media on Monday brought to his attention that the question was about random voices out there .
